recommendations.rationale: Fact table with date dimension keys (effective_date_key, expiry_date_key) used in range filters by v_policy_detail and agg_policy_monthly. Ordering low→high cardinality: effective_date_key, product_key.
recommendations.ddl: ALTER TABLE {schema}.fact_policy CLUSTER BY (effective_date_key, product_key);
recommendations.conditions: Benefit increases with table size; most impactful above ~1TB

recommendations.rationale: Fact table with date dimension keys (incident_date_key, reported_date_key) used in range filters by v_claims_analysis and agg_claims_monthly. Ordering low→high cardinality: incident_date_key, product_key.
recommendations.ddl: ALTER TABLE {schema}.fact_claims CLUSTER BY (incident_date_key, product_key);
recommendations.conditions: Benefit increases with table size; most impactful above ~1TB

recommendations.rationale: Fact table with date dimension key (payment_date_key) used in payment analysis queries. Single date key as primary cluster column.
recommendations.ddl: ALTER TABLE {schema}.fact_payments CLUSTER BY (payment_date_key, customer_key);
recommendations.conditions: Benefit increases with table size; most impactful above ~1TB

recommendations.rationale: Staging table loaded via truncate-and-reload pattern from OperationalDB. No Fail-safe needed for transitory staging data.
recommendations.ddl: CREATE OR REPLACE TRANSIENT TABLE {schema}.stg_policies (...);

recommendations.rationale: policy_number is a high-cardinality degenerate dimension used in point lookups (customer service, policy inquiry). Search optimization enables sub-second lookup on specific policy numbers.
recommendations.ddl: ALTER TABLE {schema}.fact_policy ADD SEARCH OPTIMIZATION ON EQUALITY(policy_number);

recommendations.rationale: claim_number is a high-cardinality degenerate dimension used in point lookups (claims inquiry, adjuster workflow). Search optimization enables sub-second lookup on specific claim numbers.
recommendations.ddl: ALTER TABLE {schema}.fact_claims ADD SEARCH OPTIMIZATION ON EQUALITY(claim_number);

recommendations.rationale: customer_id (natural key) used in point lookups for customer service and SCD2 MERGE operations. Search optimization speeds up exact-match queries on natural key.
recommendations.ddl: ALTER TABLE {schema}.dim_customer ADD SEARCH OPTIMIZATION ON EQUALITY(customer_id);

recommendations.rationale: Presentation views (v_policy_detail, v_claims_analysis, v_exec_kpi_summary) suggest ad-hoc analytics workload. QAS can offload selective scan portions to shared compute for unpredictable query patterns.
recommendations.ddl: ALTER WAREHOUSE {warehouse_name} SET ENABLE_QUERY_ACCELERATION = TRUE QUERY_ACCELERATION_MAX_SCALE_FACTOR = 8;
recommendations.conditions: Requires warehouse name; cost is per-query for accelerated portions

recommendations.rationale: Foreign key relationships to dim_customer (customer_key), dim_agent (agent_key), dim_product (product_key), dim_date (effective_date_key, expiry_date_key) enable BI tool join inference and Snowflake join elimination.
recommendations.conditions: Informational only — not enforced by Snowflake on standard tables

recommendations.rationale: Foreign key relationships to dim_customer (customer_key), dim_product (product_key), dim_date (incident_date_key, reported_date_key) enable BI tool join inference and Snowflake join elimination.
recommendations.conditions: Informational only — not enforced by Snowflake on standard tables

recommendations.rationale: Foreign key relationships to dim_customer (customer_key), dim_date (payment_date_key) enable BI tool join inference and Snowflake join elimination.
recommendations.ddl:
ALTER TABLE {schema}.fact_payments ADD CONSTRAINT fk_fact_payments_customer FOREIGN KEY (customer_key) REFERENCES {schema}.dim_customer (customer_key);
ALTER TABLE {schema}.fact_payments ADD CONSTRAINT fk_fact_payments_date FOREIGN KEY (payment_date_key) REFERENCES {schema}.dim_date (date_key);
recommendations.conditions: Informational only — not enforced by Snowflake on standard tables

recommendations.rationale: Primary key constraint on date_key already declared inline but adding explicit named constraint improves BI tool discovery. Also applies to dim_product, dim_agent, dim_customer PKs.
recommendations.conditions: Informational only — not enforced by Snowflake on standard tables

recommendations.rationale: INSERT ... SELECT aggregation from fact_policy with dimension joins is a single-statement pattern suitable for Dynamic Table. Note: agg_claims_monthly is NOT a candidate because it has a post-INSERT UPDATE from agg_policy_monthly (cross-aggregate enrichment).
recommendations.ddl:
CREATE OR REPLACE DYNAMIC TABLE {schema}.agg_policy_monthly
  TARGET_LAG = '1 hour'
  WAREHOUSE = {warehouse_name}
AS
SELECT
    dd_eff.year_number,
    dd_eff.month_number,
    dd_eff.quarter_number,
    COALESCE(dp.product_line, 'UNKNOWN') AS product_line,
    COALESCE(dp.lob_code, 'UNKNOWN') AS lob_code,
    COALESCE(da.channel_code, 'UNKNOWN') AS channel_code,
    COALESCE(dc.region, 'UNKNOWN') AS region,
    COALESCE(dc.state_code, 'XX') AS state_code,
    fp.status_code,
    COUNT(fp.policy_fact_key) AS policy_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN fp.status_code = 'CN' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS cancelled_policy_count,
    SUM(COALESCE(fp.annual_premium, 0)) AS total_annual_premium,
    SUM(COALESCE(fp.earned_premium_amount, 0)) AS total_earned_premium,
    SUM(COALESCE(fp.coverage_amount, 0)) AS total_coverage_amount,
    CASE WHEN COUNT(fp.policy_fact_key) > 0
         THEN ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(fp.annual_premium, 0)) / COUNT(fp.policy_fact_key), 2)
         ELSE NULL END AS avg_premium_per_policy
FROM fact_policy fp
INNER JOIN dim_date dd_eff ON dd_eff.date_key = fp.effective_date_key
INNER JOIN dim_product dp ON dp.product_key = fp.product_key
INNER JOIN dim_agent da ON da.agent_key = fp.agent_key
INNER JOIN dim_customer dc ON dc.customer_key = fp.customer_key
WHERE dd_eff.year_number >= 2015
GROUP BY ALL;
recommendations.conditions: Requires warehouse name; TARGET_LAG should be adjusted based on freshness requirements. AUTOINCREMENT (agg_key) and DEFAULT columns are dropped in Dynamic Table version.

recommendations.rationale: The ETL follows a classic batch pipeline: staging → dimensions (SCD1/SCD2) → facts → aggregates → views. This could be modernized to near-real-time with Streams on staging tables triggering Tasks for downstream refresh.
recommendations.ddl:
-- ROADMAP: Streams + Tasks modernization
-- Stream on stg_policies → Task to MERGE into dim_customer, dim_agent, dim_product
-- Stream on stg_claims → Task to INSERT into fact_claims
-- Stream on stg_payments → Task to INSERT into fact_payments
-- Root Task: refresh_staging → child: refresh_dimensions → child: refresh_facts → child: refresh_aggregates
-- Benefits: Process only changed rows, built-in exactly-once semantics via Stream offsets
recommendations.conditions: Architectural decision — requires evaluation of source system CDC capabilities and freshness requirements

recommendations.rationale: Fact table default retention (1 day) is insufficient for operational recovery. Recommend 90 days for audit trail and point-in-time analysis. Same applies to fact_claims and fact_payments.
recommendations.conditions: Requires Enterprise Edition for retention > 1 day; increases Time Travel storage cost proportionally

recommendations.rationale: SCD2 dimension tables benefit from extended retention for historical debugging and rollback of incorrect MERGE operations. Same applies to dim_agent.
recommendations.conditions: Requires Enterprise Edition for retention > 1 day

recommendations.rationale: Audit/log table used for ETL monitoring and compliance. Extended retention supports investigation of historical batch issues.
recommendations.ddl: ALTER TABLE {schema}.etl_audit_log SET DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS = 90;
recommendations.conditions: Requires Enterprise Edition for retention > 1 day
